name: David Friedman
abstract: David Friedman (born March 10, 1944, New York, United States) is an American jazz percussionist. His primary instruments are vibraphone and marimba.

concerts.label: Hubert Laws Afro-Classic Ensemble/ Randy Weston Quintet
Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Double Image

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral
about_quotes.evidence: he got us Ron Carter on bass, Jack DeJohnette on drums, and Dave Friedman on vibes... We had sort of a piecemeal rehearsal.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Karolina Strassmayer
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve played quite a lot of free music with David Friedman... And we did a whole CD
about_quotes.doc_title: Karolina Strassmayer interviewed by Monk Rowe, May 25, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: And Dave Friedman on vibes. ... And I reacted to things and I played and we did one take of things ... And it came out the album called “Odyssey of Iska.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: The drummers are Steve Gadd, Alex Acuña, Harvey Mason, Vic Firth playing tympani, and David Friedman playing xylophone, and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
